file_pattern: rap.tHHz.awp130pgrbf00.grib2 (+ .idx) under https://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/pub/data/nccf/com/rap/prod/rap.YYYYMMDD/  HH=00..23, awp130=13km CONUS Lambert grid
latest_cycle_logic: f00 for cycle HH appears ~48 min after HH:00 UTC (t20z file Last-Modified 20:47:52Z). Robust pick: for hour in [now_utc.hour, -1, -2, -3] (handle day rollover into previous rap.YYYYMMDD dir): HEAD .grib2 (HTTP 200 + Content-Length ~18.3 MB) AND .idx -> first success wins. filter_rap.pl itself returns HTTP 404 with HTML body 'data file is not present' for missing cycles, so probing via the filter also works.

grib_filter.params: dir=%2Frap.YYYYMMDD & file=rap.tHHz.awp130pgrbf00.grib2 & var_X=on... & lev_Y=on... & subregion= & leftlon=&rightlon=&toplat=&bottomlat= (negative longitudes accepted)
grib_filter.warning: dir+file WITHOUT any var_/lev_ params returns the ENTIRE 18 MB grib2 file (HTTP 200), not an HTML form
grib_filter.moore_subset_observed: 84273 bytes, 5.6 s total (2.1 s server time-to-first-byte)

idx_facts.moisture_on_pressure_levels: RH only (37 levels). DPT exists ONLY at 2 m; SPFH only at 2 m and 80 m. Compute dewpoint aloft from T+RH (MetPy dewpoint_from_relative_humidity).
idx_facts.storm_motion: USTM/VSTM at 0-6000 m above ground (single .idx line '241.1/241.2' - two fields share one GRIB message offset)

isobaricInhPa.shape: (37, y=17, x=14) for the 2x2 deg Moore box

vars.t: K (skin temp, rides along with var_TMP)

ustm.note: level coord reads 0.0 (layer metadata lost); it is the 0-6000 m layer

silent_data_loss_traps: filter_by_keys={'typeOfLevel':'heightAboveGround'} alone returns ONLY t2m/d2m/r2 and SILENTLY drops u10/v10 (conflicting level values) - must filter level=2 and level=10 separately
filter_by_keys={'typeOfLevel':'heightAboveGroundLayer'} alone returns ONLY hlcy and SILENTLY drops ustm/vstm - filter shortName per variable

grid.lon_convention: 0-360 degrees east in decoded coords (261.44..263.40 for -98.5..-96.5W) even though the filter request used negative lons
grid.point_extraction: nearest gridpoint via argmin((lat-lat0)**2 + (lon - lon0%360)**2); Moore 35.34,-97.49 -> y=8,x=7 lat=35.341 lon=262.503
